heroHeadline: From Tahuantinsuyo to the Republic: 588 Years of Peruvian History
heroSubtitle: Tracking the full arc from Pachacuti's Inca Empire (1438) through the Spanish Conquest, independence at Ayacucho, the War of the Pacific, the Shining Path insurgency, Fujimori's autogolpe and conviction, and contemporary political crisis under President Boluarte — seven presidents in ten years.
footerNote: Data sourced from CVR Informe Final (2003), official government decrees, INEI, BCRP, Inter-American Court of Human Rights, major Peruvian outlets, and academic histories. Casualty figures for 1980–2000 conflict use CVR multiple systems estimation methodology.
